demographics.living_situation: lives with sibling, who is present

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was just sitting at the table, my heart suddenly fluttered violently, and I woke up on the floor completely disoriented.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: I passed out with no warning while sitting down, and I'm really scared because my brother had a heart problem young.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden — while sitting at the table, no warning at all
symptom_narrative.duration: the blackout was very brief; I'm fully recovered now but shaken
symptom_narrative.character: my heart fluttered really hard and fast for a moment, then I blacked out and woke up on the floor
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: terrifying — I've never fainted like that before
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: I felt it in my chest, the racing, then everything went black
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: it happened once, out of nowhere, at rest
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing that I know of — I just came round on my own quickly
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: I can't link it to anything — I wasn't exercising or upset
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: just the violent fluttering right before I went down; no chest pain
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had a racing or fluttering heart on and off for about six months but I brushed it off; never actually fainted before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I feel physically okay now but I'm very anxious and don't feel safe on my own

family_history_as_reported.narrative: My brother had a sudden cardiac arrest at 28. My mum was diagnosed with atrial fibrillation in her 30s.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young adult, otherwise well; sibling is with me and watching me closely

objective_data_offered.value: patient is sitting/lying down, awake and talking in full sentences, breathing normally, looks frightened
sibling says his colour looks normal now, he is alert and oriented, no shaking or twitching was seen during the faint and he did not bite his tongue
sibling felt his pulse, says it feels around 70 and steady right now
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: manual pulse count by a family member, not a device — rate and regularity are approximate and cannot exclude an intermittent arrhythmia
